message:
perf(quake): size framebuffer to sampled stride, not declared resolution

`QuakeEngine::render` was always allocating and rasterizing into a
framebuffer matching `self.fb_width` × `self.fb_height` (e.g.
320×200), even when the painter target only sampled the framebuffer
at a coarser stride (e.g. stride=2 → only every other pixel actually
ends up on screen). The off-screen pixels were being computed and
discarded, scaling rendering cost with the declared resolution
instead of with what the terminal can actually show.

This commit introduces `target_framebuffer_dims(painter_width,
painter_height, stride)`, which:

- Returns the full declared resolution unchanged when `stride == 1`
  (the existing fast path; no behavior change for direct-render
  consumers).
- Otherwise computes `(painter_width.div_ceil(stride),
  painter_height.div_ceil(stride))` — the count of framebuffer cells
  that will actually be sampled — and clamps each axis to the
  declared `fb_width`/`fb_height` so callers can never accidentally
  inflate beyond the engine's intended resolution. Each axis is also
  pinned to ≥ 1 to avoid a zero-sized framebuffer when the painter
  is smaller than a single stride step.

The render path now resizes both the framebuffer and the renderer to
this target size before drawing, so the inner Bresenham/raster
loops only walk the cells that will actually be sampled.

Tests:

- `engine_reduced_stride_renders_only_sampled_framebuffer` plants a
  320×200 declared resolution, drives a 120×40 painter at stride=2,
  and asserts the resulting framebuffer is 120×80 — confirming both
  axes scaled with the painter target / stride and were clamped
  below the declared 320×200. Existing stride=1 tests continue to
  hit the fast-path branch unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
